title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, Delivery Limits, and Parking · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
Configure dead lettering through mutable policy where possible and verify source read, target write, exchange existence, binding, and route permissions.
Detect and bound dead-letter cycles using history, stable identity, attempt policy, route analysis, and a terminal parking destination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Dead Letter Trigger Route
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug messaging team handles case 1366 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Parking Remediation Workflow, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Parking Remediation Workflow precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Parking Remediation Workflow, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug messaging team handles case 6414 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Parking Remediation Workflow, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Moving a message to a dead-letter queue completes incident handling.
